name: Three-Onion Ragout
recipeIngredient: 2 large Vidalia onions, cut into 16 wedges
black pepper freshly ground to taste
2 medium red onions, halved and sliced 1/4 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Brush a large shallow baking sheet with half tablespoon of olive oil. Spread the onion wedges on the pan, drizzle with half tablespoon of oil and season with salt and pepper. Cover with foil and bake for about 25 minutes, or until soft but not brown.
2. Heat half tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet. Add the scallions and season with salt and pepper. Cover and cook over high heat, stirring frequently, until tender but still green, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a bowl.
3. Heat remaining half tablespoon of oil in the skillet. Add the red onions and cook over moderate heat, stirring often, until soft but not colored, about 25 minutes. Stir in vinegar and cook until nearly evaporated, about 1 minute. Add port and cook for 1 minute longer.
4. Just before serving, combine cooked onions and scallions in skillet and reheat over moderate heat. Transfer to a bowl and serve.
Notes:
The recipe can be prepared through Step 3 up to 1 day ahead; refrigerate the onions separately. Serve with grilled or roasted poultry, pork or meaty fish.
